title: Five Prizes Ranked — Which Was Hardest?

analysis.object: T905: Five Prizes Ranked — Which Was Hardest? depth analysis

rh_difficulty.description: RH: EML-2 post-proof. Breakthrough: three-constraint + A5 killed by tropical. Difficulty: finding the tropical tool.

bsd_rank1_difficulty.description: BSD rank<=1: EML-2. Breakthrough: Gross-Zagier + Kolyvagin. Difficulty: constructing Heegner points.

hodge_difficulty.description: Hodge: EML-2 post-proof. Breakthrough: formal GAGA + Hironaka. Difficulty: realizing T775 was the key.

ym_difficulty.description: YM: EML-2 post-proof. Breakthrough: Hodge moduli + Balaban completion. Difficulty: T809 (instanton vacuum EML-finite).

bsd_full_difficulty.description: BSD full: EML-2 post-proof. Breakthrough: induction + BK + tropical + Hodge descent. Difficulty: rank 2 GKS.

hardest_ranking.description: Hardest to easiest: YM ≈ BSD-full > Hodge > RH ≈ BSD-1. NS would be hardest (EML-inf).

t905_ranking.description: T905: YM and BSD-full are the hardest proved problems. All five are EML-2 post-proof. NS alone is EML-inf. Difficulty correlates with depth of the instanton/SHA/descent construction. T905.

analysis.theorem: T905: Five Prizes Ranked — Which Was Hardest? (S1185).

key_theorem: T905: Five Prizes Ranked — Which Was Hardest? (S1185).
